perturbation.original_text: As used in this Agreement, the "Confidential Information" of the Disclosing Party shall mean all information concerning or related to the business, operations, financial condition or prospects of the Disclosing Party or any of their respective Affiliates, regardless of the form in which such information appears and whether or not such information has been reduced to a tangible form, and shall specifically include (1) all information regarding the officers, directors, employees, equity holders, customers, suppliers, distributors, insurers, reinsurers, brokers, independent contractors, sales representatives and licensees of the Disclosing Party and their respective Affiliates, in each case whether present or prospective, (2) all inventions, discoveries, trade secrets, processes, techniques, methods, formulae, ideas and know-how of the Disclosing Party and their respective Affiliates, (3) all financial statements, audit reports, budgets and business plans or forecasts of the Disclosing Party and their respective Affiliates and (4) all information concerning or related to the Transaction; provided, that the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party shall not include (x) information which is or becomes generally known to the public through no act or omission of the Receiving Party and (y) information which has been or hereafter is lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a source other than the Disclosing Party (or any of their respective Affiliates or their
perturbation.changed_text: As used in this Agreement, the "Confidential Information" of the Disclosing Party shall mean only that information explicitly marked as confidential in writing and delivered in hard copy form. It shall not include any other information, regardless of its nature or how it was obtained. Specifically, it only includes (4) all information concerning or related to the Transaction; provided, that the Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party shall not include (x) information which is or becomes generally known to the public through no act or omission of the Receiving Party and (y) information which has been or hereafter is lawfully obtained by the Receiving Party from a source other than the Disclosing Party (or any of their respective Affiliates or their
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' is broad and includes various forms of information. The modified definition severely restricts what qualifies as 'Confidential Information' to only written and hard-copy data, contradicting the original intent and scope. This in-text contradiction will lead to contradiction in Section 2, which deals with Nondisclosure of Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nondisclosure of Confidential Information. Except as otherwise permitted by Section 3, the Receiving Party agrees that it will not, without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party, disclose or use for its own benefit, or that of any third party, any Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original definition of 'Confidential Information' is broad, encompassing various forms of data. The changed text drastically narrows this definition, limiting it to explicitly marked, hard-copy information and information related to the Transaction. This creates a clear contradiction with the 'Nondisclosure of Confidential Information' clause, which prohibits the disclosure or use of 'any Confidential Information' without consent. The changed definition makes it unclear what information is actually protected under the nondisclosure agreement, creating a direct conflict and inconsistency, since the original intent was much broader. According to the Perturbation Explanation, this in-text contradiction will lead to contradiction in Section 2, which deals with Nondisclosure of Confidential Information.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall be permitted to: (a) disclose Confidential Information to its officers, employees and counsel, but only to the extent reasonably necessary in order for such party to prepare, conduct and execute and deliver definitive documents for the Transaction; provided that Receiving Party shall take all such action as shall be necessary or desirable in order to ensure that each of such persons maintains the confidentiality of any Confidential Information that is so disclosed; and (b) disclose Confidential Information to the extent, but only to the extent, required by law; provided, that prior to making any disclosure pursuant to this subsection, the Receiving Party shall notify the Disclosing Party of the same, and the Disclosing Party shall have the right to participate with the Receiving Party in determining the amount and type of Confidential Information of the Disclosing Party, if any, which must be disclosed in order to comply with applicable law, including, without limitation, state and federal securities laws and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall be permitted to: (a) disclose Confidential Information to its officers, employees and counsel without any limitations or restrictions; and (b) disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by law, without any requirement to notify the Disclosing Party or allow them to participate in determining the amount or type of Confidential Information to be disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: The original text restricts the disclosure of 'Confidential Information' to what is 'reasonably necessary' and requires measures to ensure confidentiality is maintained. It also mandates notification and participation from the Disclosing Party if disclosure is legally required. The modified text removes these restrictions and notification requirements, creating a direct contradiction in Section 3, enabling unrestricted and uninformed disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Permitted Disclosures. Notwithstanding Section 2, Receiving Party shall be permitted to: (a) disclose Confidential Information to its officers, employees and counsel without any limitations or restrictions; and (b) disclose Confidential Information to the extent required by law, without any requirement to notify the Disclosing Party or allow them to participate in determining the amount or type of Confidential Information to be disclosed.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes restrictions and notification requirements for disclosing 'Confidential Information' that were present in the original text. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, also lacks these restrictions and notification requirements. The explanation states that the original text restricts the disclosure and the modified text removes these restrictions and creates a contradiction. Therefore, the change introduces a strong, direct conflict creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: If activity in respect of the Transaction shall cease without the Transaction being consummated, then, promptly after the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall return to the Disclosing Party all Confidential Information which is in tangible form and which is then in its possession (or in the possession of any of its officers, directors or employees).
perturbation.changed_text: If activity in respect of the Transaction shall cease without the Transaction being consummated, then, promptly after the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party may or may not return any Confidential Information which is in tangible form at their discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that upon the written request of the disclosing party, the receiving party *shall* return all confidential information in tangible form. The modified text changes this to the receiving party *may or may not* return said information, at their discretion. This contradicts the original text and renders the obligation of returning confidential information optional, leading to uncertainty in section 4.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Confidential Information. If activity in respect of the Transaction shall cease without the Transaction being consummated, then, promptly after the written request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party may or may not return any Confidential Information which is in tangible form at their discretion.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party "shall" return the Confidential Information upon request, while the changed text states the Receiving Party "may or may not" return the Confidential Information. This directly contradicts the original obligation and creates a strong in-text contradiction, as confirmed by the perturbation explanation. The Contradicted Text is also identical to the Changed Text, confirming that there is a conflict within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Any assignment, delegation or attempted assignment or delegation of the rights or responsibilities established under this Agreement shall be null and void without the prior written duly executed consent by the party charged.
perturbation.changed_text: Any assignment, delegation or attempted assignment or delegation of the rights or responsibilities established under this Agreement shall be freely permissible without any consent required.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause strictly forbids the assignment or delegation of rights without prior written consent. The modified clause makes assignment and delegation freely permissible, creating a complete contradiction in Section 9 regarding the transferability of rights and responsibilities under the agreement. This removes a safeguard and drastically alters the contractual landscape for both parties.
perturbation.contradicted_text: No Assignment or Delegation. Any assignment, delegation or attempted assignment or delegation of the rights or responsibilities established under this Agreement shall be freely permissible without any consent required.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that any assignment or delegation required prior written consent. The changed text explicitly states that assignment and delegation are freely permissible without any consent. The contradicted text reinforces the changed text. This creates a direct contradiction because the original text requires consent, while the changed and contradicted texts explicitly remove this requirement. This meets the criteria for a structural flaw leading to an in-text contradiction because the same clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
